title: Swapped funds are sent to `to_bank` via raw ERC20 transfer with no deposit/accounting handshake
reason: Speculative. The available source only shows a raw token transfer into `to_bank`; there is no evidence that the destination bank requires an explicit deposit hook or that passive ERC20 receipt is insufficient for its accounting.

title: Unchecked return value from external token bank balance call
reason: Not a standalone vulnerability in this code. It relies on a malicious or broken `from_bank` dependency returning false data, which is outside the demonstrated trust model.

title: Constructor makes unsafe external calls without try-catch
reason: A constructor reverting on bad parameters or incompatible external contracts is expected deployment-time behavior, not a reportable protocol vulnerability.

title: No validation of exchange output amount before transfer
reason: Duplicate of the missing slippage protection issue. The lack of `min_dy` already captures the harmful acceptance of arbitrarily poor output.

title: Hardcoded Curve pool address creates centralization risk
reason: Operational rigidity / maintainability concern, but not a concrete exploitable vulnerability causing protocol-level harm on its own.

title: No event logging for doExchange execution
reason: Observability issue only; not a security finding.

title: Potential reentrancy vulnerability in safeApprove pattern
reason: Unsupported. The function has no sensitive intermediate state to exploit via reentrancy, and the report depends on a malicious Curve/token assumption not evidenced here.

title: Unused SafeMath library functions in main contract
reason: Code quality issue only; not a security vulnerability.

title: No deadline parameter in exchange function
reason: Not a distinct vulnerability here. The meaningful economic risk is already covered by the missing slippage protection, and the function is permissionless rather than user-order driven.
